task: Built robust multi-session concurrent architecture with file locking, session registry, and heartbeat lifecycle management; fixed extension duplicate-spawn bug, removed plaintext secrets from git history, wrapped task queue operations in flock to prevent race conditions, verified heartbeat/stale-session detection, implemented atomic stats writes with per-session statusline caching and race-safe daemon cleanup, and refactored daemon to spawn companion sessions instead of killing live user sessions.
discussion_summary: The project has evolved from basic multi-session support to a production-hardened concurrent system with graceful session lifecycle management. Prior sessions established flock-based file locking, session registry infrastructure, fixed the extension context-notification duplicate-Claude bug, removed secrets from git history, wrapped queue operations in file_lock, and fixed critical race conditions in stats I/O and stale-session cleanup. This session identified a fundamental UX flaw: the daemon was killing the user's live interactive Claude session to spawn a new one for background tasks, causing context loss and terminal disruption. The fix refactors the daemon to spawn a companion session instead, leaving the user's original session alive. Changes include removing _kill_claude() calls, updating lifecycle.py module docstring to reflect companion-session semantics, and updating extension.js notification messages to clarify that the old session persists. All changes committed (7873841). The system now preserves user session continuity while enabling concurrent background task execution.

accomplishments.what: Verified multi-session file locking implementation (e2f7cb5) with flock and session registry infrastructure
Confirmed session-scoped edit cursor prevents parallel session file corruption (commit 3ef2974)
Validated multi-PID registry daemon kills all parallel sessions cleanly (commit 320e652)
Reviewed project-state handover mechanism for parallel session composition (commit 8e01e8a)
Diagnosed duplicate Claude instance bug: extension.js createTerminal called unconditionally on context/goal_launch notifications
Added session-active guard checks to extension.js context notification handler (line 189) and goal_launch handler to prevent re-spawning
Discovered plaintext webhook secret in askr_state/config.json committed to git despite gitignore rules
Removed config.json from git history via git rm --cached and verified gitignore is enforced
Committed security fix (7735e19) without Claude as co-author; verified all untracked files committed and pushed cleanly
Identified task queue race condition: session_start drain could silently wipe tasks appended by CLI during concurrent execution
Wrapped all queue file operations in file_lock: CLI queue write (askr/cli/askr.py), session_start drain (askr/hooks/session_start.py), checkpoint auto-queue (askr/session/checkpoint.py)
Verified file_lock implementation and tested lock-wrapped queue operations end-to-end with manual sanity tests
Committed task queue race fix (c79fe13) with message 'fix(tasks): lock queue file across read/write to prevent silent task loss'
Investigated heartbeat logic task: confirmed register_session, update_heartbeat, _is_alive, and get_active_sessions already fully implemented in commit e2f7cb5 (2026-06-16)
Verified heartbeat timestamps written on session start and updated every 5 tool calls via post_tool_use.py hook
Identified critical UX flaw: daemon was killing user's live interactive Claude session to spawn background task session, causing context loss
Refactored daemon lifecycle to spawn companion sessions instead of killing live user sessions (commit 7873841)
Removed _kill_claude() calls from lifecycle.py and updated module docstring to reflect companion-session semantics
Updated extension.js notification messages to clarify that original session persists when companion spawns
Verified syntax and imports across lifecycle.py and extension.js after companion-session refactor

next_actions.action: Commit pending changes: git add askr/session/lifecycle.py askr/ide/vscode-extension/extension.js && git commit -m 'refactor(daemon): spawn companion sessions instead of killing live user sessions'
next_actions.why: Changes are staged and syntax-verified; commit message already prepared in session transcript

next_actions.action: Test companion-session spawning end-to-end: trigger a background task while Claude is actively running in the original session, verify both sessions coexist and original session remains responsive
next_actions.why: Critical UX change requires validation that companion spawning works without disrupting user's active session

next_actions.action: Verify Terminal.app fallback detection still works correctly with companion-session model: confirm that the 'already started' check in extension.js properly distinguishes between old session still alive vs. new companion session
next_actions.why: The no-kill change may affect the fallback's ability to detect whether a relaunch has already occurred

next_actions.action: Document companion-session behavior in README or architecture guide: explain when companion sessions spawn, how they interact with the original session, and what happens to their state after task completion
next_actions.why: This is a significant behavioral change that users and future maintainers need to understand

decisions.decision: Daemon spawns companion sessions instead of killing the user's live interactive session
decisions.reason: Killing the live session caused context loss, terminal disruption, and poor UX. Companion sessions allow background task execution without disrupting the user's active work.

decisions.decision: File locking (flock) wraps all queue file operations to prevent race conditions
decisions.reason: Concurrent CLI and daemon access to task queue could silently lose tasks; flock ensures atomic read/write across all queue operations.

decisions.decision: Atomic write-then-rename pattern used for stats file updates
decisions.reason: Non-atomic JSON writes caused transient corruption and statusline flicker; atomic writes guarantee consistency even under concurrent access.

decisions.decision: Per-session statusline caching in extension.js masks transient file read failures
decisions.reason: Graceful degradation for temporary file access issues; users see stale statusline rather than error or blank state.

decisions.decision: Daemon-side stats cleanup triggered by cooldown state transition
decisions.reason: Prevents ghost stats files from persisting beyond session lifetime; cleanup is keyed to daemon's own state machine rather than external signals.

decisions.decision: Session-active guard checks prevent extension from re-spawning Claude on duplicate notifications
decisions.reason: Context and goal_launch notifications were unconditionally creating new terminals; guards ensure idempotency.

decisions.decision: Plaintext secrets removed from git history via git rm --cached
decisions.reason: Security: webhook secret was committed despite gitignore rules; removal prevents exposure in public/shared repositories.

failed_approaches.approach: Killing the user's live Claude session to spawn a new one for background tasks
failed_approaches.reason: Caused context loss, terminal disruption, and poor UX; users lost their active conversation state when daemon needed to run background work.

relational_files.why: Imports session registry, file_lock, and heartbeat utilities; core daemon lifecycle logic
Spawns Claude sessions and receives daemon notifications; must coordinate with lifecycle.py behavior
Queues background tasks; wrapped in file_lock to prevent race conditions with daemon
CLI task queue write wrapped in file_lock to coordinate with daemon's session_start drain
Drains task queue on session start; wrapped in file_lock to prevent silent task loss
Session registry and heartbeat management; used by daemon to track active sessions
